info.title: Axon REST API
info.description: Dedicated REST routes for Axon discovery, RAG, crawl, ingest, and watch workflows.

200.description: SQLite, Qdrant, and TEI dependencies are ready

503.description: One or more dependencies are not ready

get.summary: Serve an artifact file from the configured output directory.
get.description:
The `path` query parameter is validated structurally and via canonicalization
before serving the file. Returns:
- `400` for structurally unsafe paths (absolute, `..`, etc.)
- `403` when the resolved path escapes the output root or is a symlink
- `404` when the file does not exist or is not a regular file

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Slash-preserving relative path within the output directory, e.g. `jobs/abc/output.md`

502.description: Upstream vector or LLM service unavailable

200.description: RAG answer streamed as server-sent events
Extracted brand identity from a URL
Direct LLM chat answer streamed as server-sent events
Markdown, metadata, and link diff between two URLs

502.description: Upstream fetch or verification service unavailable
Upstream LLM or vector service unavailable

200.description: Supported node/edge/evidence kinds and authority levels
Resolved graph nodes for each identifier
Graph nodes/edges tied to the source
Unified job events streamed as server-sent events

502.description: Upstream vector or embedding service unavailable
Upstream vector or embedding service unavailable
Upstream vector or embedding service unavailable

post.summary:
`POST /v1/memories/export` — export memory records matching a scope, with
the same body-size layer as `import_memories` (the request body is small,
but the response can be large; the size limit bounds abusive requests
consistently across both routes).

413.description: Export request payload exceeds the size limit

502.description: Upstream vector or embedding service unavailable

post.summary:
`POST /v1/memories/import` — bulk-import memory records with an explicit
body size limit (`large_write_routes` layers `DefaultBodyLimit::max` on
this route in `routing.rs`; axum's `Json` extractor maps the resulting
oversized-body rejection to `413 Payload Too Large`).

413.description: Import payload exceeds the size limit

502.description: Upstream vector or embedding service unavailable
Upstream vector or embedding service unavailable
Upstream vector or embedding service unavailable
Upstream vector or embedding service unavailable

delete.summary:
`DELETE /v1/memories/{memory_id}` — forget. `reason` is accepted as an
optional JSON body for parity with the other id-scoped mutation routes.

502.description: Upstream vector or embedding service unavailable
Upstream vector or embedding service unavailable
Upstream vector or embedding service unavailable
Upstream vector or embedding service unavailable

post.summary:
`POST /v1/memories/{memory_id}/link` — `source_id` is filled from the path
(the memory being linked), `target_id` from the body.

502.description: Upstream vector or embedding service unavailable
Upstream vector or embedding service unavailable
Upstream vector or embedding service unavailable
Upstream vector or embedding service unavailable
Upstream vector or embedding service unavailable

200.description: Prune execution result (receipt of what was actually deleted)

400.description: Invalid prune request, or missing confirm=true

Research synthesis streamed as server-sent events
Search results and queued crawl jobs

202.description: Source indexing enqueued as a detached job

403.description: Source not authorized for caller scopes

404.description: Source not found in the ledger

503.description: Source ledger not configured on this deployment

429.description: Upstream LLM or search provider rate limited

502.description: Upstream search or LLM service unavailable

200.description: Brief LLM summary of scraped URL content

502.description: Upstream crawl, render, or LLM service unavailable

200.description: Brief LLM summary streamed as server-sent events

ApiError.description:
The shared, transport-neutral error shape.

Every crate reports failures as an `ApiError` so CLI, REST, MCP, jobs, logs,
and progress streams render one consistent structure.

details.description: Redacted structured context (safe key/value pairs only).

visibility.description: Where this error may be surfaced.

text_diff.description: Unified diff of the markdown content, if any changes.

mcp_candidates.description: Synthesized MCP candidate probe attempts (omitted when empty).

ErrorSeverity.description: How severe an error is and whether it terminates the item/job.

ErrorStage.description: Pipeline/transport stage an error is attributed to.

ErrorVisibility.description:
Where a context entry (or error) may be surfaced.

From the "visibility" field in the error shape: `public`, `internal`,
`sensitive`.

actor.description:
User/system identity requesting the cancellation, echoed back on
`JobCancelResult::canceled_by`.

canceled_by.description:
User/system identity that requested the cancellation.
Per `docs/pipeline-unification/runtime/job-contract.md` "Cancellation".

cleanup_debt_ids.description: Cleanup work created for any published partial side effect.

oneOf.description: Last completed safe point (stage phase) before cancellation unwound.

side_effects.description: Published/written side effects that survived the cooperative unwind.

value.description:
**Display-formatted, not machine-readable.** Pre-rendered for direct
rendering by clients — integers carry thousands separators (`"1,024"`)
and string fields (e.g. ingest `phase`) pass through verbatim. Do not
parse this back into a number; if a surface needs the raw value, add a
typed field rather than reverse-engineering the formatting here.

JobProgress.description: Derived, transport-neutral progress for a generic async job.

percent.description: 0–100 when determinate; `None` = indeterminate (render a pulsing bar).

JobStatusResponse.description:
Typed job-status envelope so the `{ job, progress }` wire shape is a
registered OpenAPI schema (and thus reflected into the generated palette/
android clients) instead of an opaque `serde_json::Value`.

job.description:
Raw job record in the wire-compat shape (`status`, `result_json`,
timestamps, …). Still `Value` because the per-family job payloads are
heterogeneous; `progress` is the typed, cross-family projection of it.

oneOf.description:
Server-derived, transport-neutral progress for the generic async
families (embed/extract/ingest). `None` for crawl, which carries a
richer client-side snapshot rather than the generic shape.

McpCandidateAttempt.description:
One synthesized MCP candidate and its probe outcome.

`rpc_probe` is `Some` iff `outcome == Confirmed`; every other outcome
(`Unconfirmed`, `Blocked`) carries `None`.

McpHostKind.description: Which host a synthesized MCP candidate targets.

McpProbeOutcome.description: Outcome of probing one synthesized MCP candidate.

MemoryDecayPolicy.description:
Explicit, inspectable decay configuration.

Contract: "Decay Contract" required fields.

MemoryExportRequest.description:
Export memory records matching a scope. Contract: "Memory DTOs" —
`MemoryExportRequest`; REST `GET /v1/memories/export`.

include_working.description:
Include `working`-status memories in the export. Contract "Import and
Export" implies the "Type rules" exclusion ("working memories are
excluded from long-term exports by default") applies to export, not
just ask/context; defaults to `false` to match that default.

oneOf.description:
Artifact backing this export, when written through the artifact
boundary (contract "Import and Export": "export writes an artifact or
stream with redacted content according to caller scope"). `None` when
the caller only requested the in-memory record list (e.g. a direct
`axon-memory` store call with no artifact root configured).

MemoryHistoryEvent.description: Append-only history event recorded on every status/scoring change.

MemoryImportMode.description: How an import reconciles with existing memories for the same content.

MemoryImportRequest.description:
Bulk-import memory records. Contract: "Memory DTOs" —
`MemoryImportRequest`; REST `POST /v1/memories/import`.

Carries records directly rather than an artifact/upload bundle
reference — no bundle serialization format is specified anywhere in the
pipeline-unification docs. A transport that wants to accept an uploaded
bundle can deserialize it into `records` itself; axon-memory does not own
artifact/upload storage (out of this crate's boundary).

MemoryImportResult.description: Result of a memory import (or dry-run plan).

created_ids.description:
Memory ids actually inserted (empty on a dry run). Lets a decorator
(e.g. vector embedding) act on exactly what was created without a
second lookup.

MemoryLink.description: Evidence-backed link from a memory to a source/entity/decision.

MemoryRecord.description: Full stored memory record. Contract: "Memory DTOs" — `MemoryRecord`.

oneOf.description: Memory this record conflicts with (`contradicted` status only).
Memory this record was replaced by (`superseded` status only).

visibility.description:
Security classification (contract "Security and Redaction": "classify
every memory by visibility"). Drives caller-scope filtering on
export/context (e.g. `sensitive` records excluded from export unless
the caller is authorized) independent of `MemoryStatus`.

MemoryScope.description:
Memory scope controlling recall and visibility.

Contract: "Scope Model" table (`global`, `project`, `repo`, `file`,
`source_id`, `graph_node_id`, `agent`, `user`, `environment`).

MemoryStatus.description:
Closed set of memory recall statuses.

Contract: "Memory Status" table — `active`, `review`, `superseded`,
`contradicted`, `archived`, `forgotten`, `working`.

MemoryType.description:
Closed set of durable memory kinds.

Contract: "Memory Types" table — `decision`, `fact`, `preference`, `task`,
`bug`, `procedure`, `incident`, `entity`, `episode`, `working`.

detail.description: Raw per-provider diagnostics payload from the doctor report.

ok.description: `true` when the doctor probe for this provider reported healthy.

PruneCounts.description: Actual deletion counts from an executed prune (the receipt's tallies).

PruneEstimate.description: Estimated impact counts for a plan (what *would* be deleted).

PruneExecRequest.description:
Body for `POST /v1/prune/exec` — destructive; requires `axon:admin` (see
[`super::super::routing::admin_routes`]) and `confirm: true`.

PrunePlan.description:
A resolved, reviewable plan describing exactly what a prune would delete.

A plan is produced by dry-run resolution and never mutates state. It must be
serializable to JSON for review (safety rule: "prune plans must be
reviewable as JSON").

PrunePlanRequest.description:
Body for `POST /v1/prune/plan` — a dry-run plan, always safe to call.

`target` is either a bare source id or `collection:<name>` for a
whole-collection prune, mirroring the CLI's `axon prune plan <target>`
selector grammar (`crates/axon-cli/src/commands/prune.rs::build_selector`).

PruneResult.description: The outcome of executing a prune plan.

oneOf.description: Prune one generation of a source (generation-fenced).
Prune a single artifact by id (never by arbitrary path).
Job-retention cleanup older than N days.
Cache cleanup older than N days.

PruneSelector.description:
The scope of a prune. Each variant resolves to a concrete plan by the
`axon-prune` planner. Variants mirror the pruning contract exactly.

PruneStep.description:
One ordered step in a prune plan. Steps follow the cleanup-debt execution
order (vector → artifact → graph → memory → ledger → job/cache).

graph_edge_ids.description:
Identity for a [`PruneTargetKind::Graph`] edge-delete step, matching
`GraphStore::delete_edges`. Additive.

graph_stable_keys.description:
Identity for a [`PruneTargetKind::Graph`] node-delete step (stable
keys, matching [`axon_graph`]'s `GraphStore::delete_nodes`). Additive —
existing `Vector`/`Ledger` steps never set this.

memory_ids.description:
Identity for a [`PruneTargetKind::Memory`] forget step, matching
`MemoryStore::forget`. Additive.

PruneStepResult.description:
Per-step execution outcome. Records what was actually deleted plus any
skipped reason, satisfying the receipt requirement.

PruneTargetKind.description: The store boundary a prune step targets, in cleanup-debt execution order.

dry_run.description:
Preview only — count matches without deleting. **Defaults to `true`** for
agent safety: a bare `purge` previews; set `dry_run=false` to delete.

prefix.description: Match `target` as a prefix over a whole docs subtree / origin.

target.description:
URL (or seed-URL/origin when `prefix` is set) to delete from the index.
**Handler-required despite the `Option`:** the type is `Option<String>`
only so a missing field deserializes to a clean "target is required"
error instead of a serde rejection; `handle_purge` returns an error when
it is `None`. It is not an optional argument.

PurgeResult.description:
Result of a purge: counts of points/URLs matched (and deleted, unless this
was a `dry_run` preview).

dry_run.description: When true, nothing was deleted — counts reflect what *would* be removed.

RpcProbeResult.description:
Result of probing a discovered endpoint for JSON-RPC 2.0 / OpenRPC / MCP support.

Fields are populated according to the detected `protocol` and are otherwise
left empty: `server_name`/`server_version`/`tools` are MCP-only, `methods` is
JSON-RPC/OpenRPC-only. The flat shape preserves the wire contract; the type
does not statically prevent contradictory combinations.

oneOf.description: Detected protocol, or `null` when no protocol matched.
Transport the protocol was observed over.

RpcProtocol.description:
Detected RPC protocol family. Wire strings match the historical
stringly-typed values (`"jsonrpc2"`, `"openrpc"`, `"mcp"`).

RpcTransport.description: Transport layer the protocol was observed over.

supported_actions.description:
Legacy internal action names retained for the panel command path.

Public HTTP clients should use `supported_routes`; `/v1/actions` is no
longer mounted after the direct REST cutover.

StreamEvent.description:
The contracted flat SSE/MCP streaming envelope. `data` carries the
kind-specific payload (a `SourceProgressEvent` for `progress`, the
route-specific result DTO for `final`, a `{ "text": ... }` object for
`token`, etc.) — see the contract doc for the per-kind validation rules.

StreamKind.description:
`StreamEvent.kind` — the flat set of streaming event kinds shared by SSE
and MCP streaming, per `docs/pipeline-unification/schemas/event-schema.md`
("StreamEvent Shape").

tags.name: discovery
tags.description: Read-only source, domain, stats, status, and health endpoints

tags.name: rag
tags.description: Query, retrieve, ask, evaluate, and suggest endpoints

tags.name: exploration
tags.description: Summarize, map, search, and research endpoints
